commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The PR has no narrow automation repair target; the remaining decision is maintainer acceptance of compatibility-sensitive package-manager precedence and whether to require full packaged-install smoke before merge.; Cleared: The diff changes local package-manager detection and tests only; it does not add dependencies, alter lockfiles, broaden secrets, or introduce new code execution paths. (sha=fd8dd4fd0cedf6abc398dad39dec8f332062da5b)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes update/status package-manager detection so npm package roots with `npm-shrinkwrap.json` report npm while pnpm and Bun-owned roots retain their existing manager-specific ownership and dependency markers.

PR surface: Source +75, Tests +248. Total +323 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. The current-main source path selects `package.json` `packageManager` before looking at install evidence, while the linked user report shows a published npm package root with `npm-shrinkwrap.json` and build-time pnpm metadata being reported as pnpm.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The PR has no narrow automation repair target; the remaining decision is maintainer acceptance of compatibility-sensitive package-manager precedence and whether to require full packaged-install smoke before merge.

target.title: fix(update): detect npm shrinkwrap package installs
target.body:
## Summary
- treat `npm-shrinkwrap.json` as npm install evidence for published npm package roots, even when package.json carries build-time `packageManager: pnpm`
- preserve pnpm package-root ownership when pnpm global/project layout proves it owns a package root that also carries `npm-shrinkwrap.json`
- preserve pnpm sibling virtual-store roots shaped like `.../5/.pnpm/.../node_modules/openclaw` by mapping them back to the owning `.../5/node_modules` layout
- preserve Bun global package-root ownership even when the published package metadata still says `pnpm`
- use `npm-shrinkwrap.json` as the npm dependency lock marker when present so `openclaw status --json` does not report a missing pnpm/package-lock marker

Fixes #87732.

## Surface area
- No public config changes.
- No plugin API or plugin contract changes.
- No update command behavior rewrite; this only changes status/install detection and dependency marker reporting.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
A package-style OpenClaw install that contains `npm-shrinkwrap.json`, `node_modules`, and build-time package metadata should be reported using the package manager that owns the install root. npm shrinkwrap package roots should report npm, while pnpm and Bun-owned package roots should remain pnpm or Bun even when the published package also contains shrinkwrap and `packageManager: pnpm`.

Real environment tested:
WSL2 Ubuntu checkout at `/tmp/openclaw-pr-87778-refresh`, PR head `fd8dd4fd0ced`, source-level temp package roots using the real `detectPackageManager` and `checkUpdateStatus` code paths.

Exact command run after this patch:
`node --import tsx ./.proof-87778.ts`

Evidence after fix:
```json
{
  "status": "pass",
  "head": "fd8dd4fd0ced",
  "managers": {
    "npm": {
      "detected": "npm",
      "installKind": "package",
      "packageManager": "npm",
      "deps": {
        "manager": "npm",
        "status": "ok",
        "lockfile": "npm-shrinkwrap.json",
        "marker": "node_modules"
      }
    },
    "pnpmDirect": {
      "detected": "pnpm",
      "installKind": "package",
      "packageManager": "pnpm",
      "deps": {
        "manager": "pnpm",
        "status": "ok",
        "lockfile": "pnpm-lock.yaml",
        "marker": ".modules.yaml"
      }
    },
    "pnpmSiblingStore": {
      "detected": "pnpm",
      "installKind": "package",
      "packageManager": "pnpm",
      "deps": {
        "manager": "pnpm",
        "status": "ok",
        "lockfile": "pnpm-lock.yaml",
        "marker": ".modules.yaml"
      }
    },
    "bun": {
      "detected": "bun",
      "installKind": "package",
      "packageManager": "bun",
      "deps": {
        "manager": "bun",
        "status": "ok",
        "lockfile": "bun.lockb",
        "marker": "node_modules"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix:
The npm temp package root detected npm and used `npm-shrinkwrap.json` as the npm lockfile. The direct pnpm global package root detected pnpm. The pnpm sibling virtual-store root `.../6/.pnpm/openclaw@1.0.0/node_modules/openclaw` also detected pnpm by using the owning `.../6/node_modules` markers. The Bun-owned temp package root used published `packageManager: pnpm` metadata plus `npm-shrinkwrap.json`, but still detected Bun from the Bun global install root and used Bun dependency markers.

What was not tested:
No registry lookup was tested; this proof disables registry access with `includeRegistry: false` because the bug is local install/dependency-marker detection.

## Validation
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/detect-package-manager.test.ts src/infra/update-check.test.ts src/infra/update-global.test.ts src/commands/status.update.test.ts src/commands/status-json-payload.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 5 files, 74 tests passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/infra/detect-package-manager.ts src/infra/detect-package-manager.test.ts` -> passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig ./tsconfig.json src/infra/detect-package-manager.ts src/infra/detect-package-manager.test.ts` -> 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `git diff --check`
- `codex review --base origin/main` -> no actionable regressions found

Rebased onto current `main` (`e18099b8c32420fa2ff5eddf8731e24c7278851b`). All 5 commits are signed. Signed head: `fd8dd4fd0cedf6abc398dad39dec8f332062da5b`.
